new_classes.label_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky, in containers holding > 2 l (CN 2208 30 49)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Malt Scotch Whisky, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 2208 30 49)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky produced by blending two or more malt Scotch whiskies distilled at different distilleries, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres. Corresponds to CN code 2208 30 49.

coverage_explanation: The legal text identifies the following discriminating criteria for CN 2208 30 49: (1) the product is a "blended malt" whisky — captured via BlendedMaltWhiskyQuality and BlendedMaltWhiskyProduction; (2) it is specifically "Blended Malt Scotch" whisky, meaning it is produced in Scotland by blending two or more malt Scotch whiskies from different distilleries — captured via ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality; (3) it is presented in containers holding more than 2 litres — captured via hasPart some LargeContainer (≥ 2 L). The existing TBox LargeContainer is defined as ≥ 2 L, which covers > 2 L in practice for the distinction from SmallContainer (< 2 L). A small gap (0.1 reduction) remains because the exact boundary semantics of "containers > 2 L" vs "≥ 2 L" in LargeContainer is not perfectly modelled (the existing LargeContainer uses minInclusive 2.0, which technically includes 2.0 L; however, the sibling 22083041 uses SmallContainer defined as strictly < 2 L, so the threshold is correctly discriminated). The complement restriction against the sibling class 22083041 (small container) is included to make the mutual exclusion explicit.
